content:
Imagine your body is like a castle, and inside this castle, you have tiny soldiers called your immune system. These soldiers protect you from germs that can make you sick. Sometimes, new germs try to sneak into the castle, and your soldiers don’t know how to fight them yet.

A vaccine is like a special training lesson for your soldiers. It gives them a tiny, harmless piece of a germ—so small it can’t hurt you. When your soldiers see this piece, they learn how to fight the germ really well. It’s like showing them a picture of the bad guy before the bad guy arrives.

So, if the real germ tries to get into your body later, your soldiers are ready! They recognize the germ and can stop it quickly, keeping you healthy. Vaccines help your body practice being strong and safe without having to get sick first!
